items.source: No EB/DM confirmed in any call, Next Step note, or contact role
Leslie McElhaney coordinates RFP logistics only; no advocacy evidence
No budget or pricing discussion found in the 26 Feb 2026 call or any Next Step entry

OpportunityContactRole shows 0 records against 9 known account-level contacts (Derek Griffing, Josh Ullrich, Chris Ward, Saleem Lakhani, Ashley Looney, Ken Bianchi, Bobby Knight, Michael Blair, Minh Le)

metrics.narrative: Josh Ullrich described the current pain in detail on the 26 Feb 2026 call: incidents are logged in a 'web based, kind of task management system' that emails a predefined chain, and 'if the communication goes off of that specific system generated email, it kinda disappears from the flow.' Leslie McElhaney added that 'incident and supplier performance' are 'tracked manually in Excel scorecards until system implementation.' No quantified cost of this pain (time, dollars, incident volume) was ever voiced, and no ROI or business-case metric has been discussed since.

champion.narrative: Leslie McElhaney (unconfirmed champion; RFP coordination only) has driven RFP logistics — she runs the Symphony eSourcing distribution, communicated the RFP timeline slips, and coordinated the demo agenda — but has shown no advocacy behavior for Zycus specifically (no internal selling, no proactive follow-up, no re-engagement in 132+ days). No one else on the buying side has stepped into a champion role.

competition.narrative: The only concretely evidenced alternative is the status quo: Josh Ullrich's current web-based task/email system and Leslie McElhaney's manual Excel scorecarding, both described as the process Zycus would replace. Leslie also referenced an unnamed set of 'companies... very heavily on FSIM or... supplier governance' that are not full source-to-pay providers, without naming any of them — this cannot be turned into named competitor entries. Separately, Zycus self-withdrew from a related, first-phase Supplier Incident Management RFP on 12 Mar 2026 ('fitment gaps too high, no longer responding to this RFP' — Next_Step__c), an unresolved signal that similar fit concerns could resurface in the full S2P evaluation.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is reasonably well articulated but not yet fully owned by a confirmed buyer champion: Josh Ullrich detailed the broken incident-escalation email workflow, and Leslie McElhaney named manual Excel-based scorecarding as a parallel pain point she is 'doing... right now with my team.' Both were confirmed as current-state facts on the 26 Feb 2026 call, but neither has re-engaged since to validate or expand on it.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting, legal, or paper-process discussion has occurred. The deal is still pre-RFP-release at the Qualified stage; paper process is not yet a live topic.

sources: Account contact list (Chris Ward, Michael Blair, Minh Le — no opp-level engagement evidence)
economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is identified or engaged. Gina Rendar joined briefly on the 26 Feb 2026 call, referenced having led a 'commercial organization' at a prior software company and having been introduced to Zycus 'years ago,' but she left after ~13 minutes saying 'I'm gonna drop off then because I don't need to see all this... let Leslie and Josh continue,' and has not reappeared on any subsequent call. Her title is not recorded in Salesforce contact data. Chris Ward (Chief Supply Chain Officer), Michael Blair (Strategy Officer) and Minh Le are known account-level contacts but show no evidence of engagement on this opportunity.

decision_process.narrative: Grace Kim (Zycus) described the process on the call: 'post RFP, you know, after we send our response, if we're down selected, we'd be able to meet with both of you.' Leslie McElhaney confirmed a down-select flow after RFP submission with demos for finalists, and targeted 'I'd... call it Q3 to have a decision made,' with implementation potentially kicked off in Q4. This process has not been re-confirmed since 26 Feb 2026, and the RFP itself (the trigger for the whole sequence) is currently overdue.

decision_criteria.narrative: Leslie McElhaney explained on the 26 Feb 2026 call that the RFP itself will define criteria: 'the RFP, it's gonna have a bunch of questions in there... our requirements will be really clear on kinda what we're looking for.' She also flagged a live evaluation question of single-vendor end-to-end fit versus a point solution: 'there's a blend of folks in our list that are companies that are very heavily on FSIM or... supplier governance space, but they're not true end-to-end spend management... the decision we have to make is you go with a system that's gonna integrate with spend management.'

items.name: Close date pushed ~6 weeks
items.summary: Close date moved from 2026-07-14 to 2026-08-27, a meaningful slip that signals delay on the buyer side or a timeline reset; no accompanying note explains the cause.

day_summary.overall: The Checkers - Rally's close date got pushed roughly six weeks, from late July to end of August. That's the only recorded activity today — no note explaining the reason, but a slip of this size usually means either a stalled internal process on their side or a re-scoping of the deal that isn't reflected yet. We're not shown as waiting on a specific deliverable, so the ball is effectively in our court to find out why and re-engage.

cro_panel.intro: A single, well-documented discovery/demo call from 26 Feb 2026 shows real but narrow interest (incident management specifically) inside a still-unreleased, three-times-delayed RFP process, with no economic buyer, no confirmed champion, no budget conversation, and no verified buyer contact in the 132 days since

reasons.summary: Scope shrinking vs prior — The Zycus team acknowledged missing the full-suite scope in the meeting invite and narrowed the live demo to iSupplier/iRisk, promising a recorded full-suite video and a future live walkthrough instea. Buyer likely getting defensive on cost or implementation/integration (phased over big-bang) — watch.

critical_signals.text: The only call on record, 26 Feb 2026 (about 4.5 months ago), was narrowed on the fly after a scoping miscommunication: the buyer wanted a full spend-management suite overview but got only supplier-governance and incident-management, and the senior attendee left the call after about 13 minutes saying she didn't need to see the rest.

The buyer is currently running the process on manual Excel scorecards and an email-based incident system that loses replies outside the tool, which is the real alternative Zycus is up against right now, not a named vendor.

A senior, commercially-experienced attendee joined the 26 Feb 2026 call, expressed she'd been introduced to Zycus years earlier and was curious to see the current offering, but has not been heard from since and her role isn't confirmed anywhere in Salesforce.
No budget, pricing, or approval conversation has ever taken place on this deal; the $125k figure in the system has not been validated with any buyer.
The buyer asked for a recorded, full end-to-end suite demo video as the bridge to a follow-up session, but there is no evidence it was ever delivered or watched.

north_star_verdict.math: 146 days at Qualified since creation (11 Feb 2026), no stage advance. Close date has slipped twice: 25 May 2026 -> 14 Jul 2026 (+50 days, moved 11 May 2026) -> 27 Aug 2026 (+44 days, moved 15 Jun 2026), a combined 94-day slip from the earliest recorded target. Last confirmed two-way buyer exchange was 4 Jun 2026 (33 days ago); the most recent rep follow-up (22 Jun 2026, 15 days ago) awaiting the 'first week of July' RFP release has gone unanswered. 51 days remain to the current 27 Aug close date, and the RFP that would trigger real evaluation has still not been confirmed released.

Next_Step_History__c 2026-06-22: 'awaiting RFP release first week of July' (no further update logged as of 7 Jul 2026)
OpportunityFieldHistory: StageName Initial Interest -> Qualified same day as creation (11 Feb 2026), no subsequent stage movement
north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is single-threaded to two operational Checkers contacts awaiting a Source-to-Pay RFP that has slipped three times and is now overdue past its last-stated window, with no economic buyer or champion in place.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (RFP not yet confirmed released, 0 contact roles, no EB or champion identified; Best Case is not supportable at Qualified stage 51 days out)

flags: Re-engage the buyer directly by phone (not email) — the deal has been silent for 129 days and the RFP is a week overdue; if no reply in 5 business days, disqualify or move to nurture.

Quantify the business case — the buyer described operational pain (lost incident emails, manual Excel tracking) but never stated the cost, FTE burden, or savings; build the ROI to justify the $125k investment.

A third-party advisory firm (Maine Pointe) is running a structured cost/timeline scoring process; get the actual evaluation criteria directly from Josh or Leslie so Zycus's S2P submission is built to the real rubric, not written blind after release.

The S2P RFP release has slipped three times (end of April, May, first week of July) and is now overdue as of 9 Jul 2026 with no buyer acknowledgement; a direct status ask is the highest-priority next action.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal is single-threaded, has an unresolved fit-perception risk from a related withdrawn RFP, and has no confirmed buyer response to a repeatedly-slipping RFP timeline.

win_position.text: No economic buyer or decision maker has been identified or engaged at any point (26 Feb 2026 call and account contact review), which caps how confidently this can be read as winnable regardless of stage.

The only two confirmed buyer contacts, Leslie McElhaney and Josh Ullrich, have not responded to any outreach since the 26 Feb 2026 demo despite the rep's dated follow-ups on 12 Mar, 17 Mar, 30 Mar, 13 Apr, 26 May, 4 Jun, and 22 Jun 2026.
Zycus self-withdrew from a related, earlier Supplier Incident Management RFP on 12 Mar 2026 citing fitment gaps too high, an open question mark for the broader S2P evaluation still pending.

The buyer has articulated a real, specific pain (a lossy email-based incident workflow and manual Excel scorecarding) that maps directly to the iSupplier/Merlin Intake products in scope, per Josh Ullrich and Leslie McElhaney on the 26 Feb 2026 call.

deal_momentum.text: 132 days have passed since the last verified buyer activity (27 Feb 2026 per LastActivityDate), against a Qualified stage that should move on a much shorter cadence.

The S2P RFP release date has slipped three times (end of April, then May, then first week of July per the dated Next Step trail through 22 Jun 2026) and is now overdue as of 9 Jul 2026 with no buyer update.
All recent activity on the deal is rep-initiated outreach logged in Next_Step_History__c (most recently 22 Jun 2026); there is no evidence of a two-way buyer response in that period.

The senior attendee on the 26 Feb 2026 call disengaged from the demo itself, asking to be looped back in only once a tailored platform walkthrough is scheduled.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Zycus led with Merlin Agentic AI throughout the demo (AI-driven supplier onboarding/OCR extraction, Questgenie for KPI question generation, embedded AI across modules). The buyer did not push back on AI but also did not raise it as a specific evaluation driver.

items.quote: I missed this part of it, in the email invite... what we wanted to see was a full breadth of the offering... It wasn't just covering incident management.
items.criterion: Full source-to-pay suite overview (not just incident management)

items.quote: Completely dynamic. So one thing that Zycus does... is that we do provide dynamic workflows and routing logic.
items.criterion: Customizable, dynamic incident-management workflow with routing logic

items.quote: This is where you can create your own weightage model. You can assign different weightages to all the different questions.
items.criterion: Weighted KPI/scorecard model tied to incident tracking

customer_expectations_fit.summary: The 26 Feb 2026 demo only partially matched what the buyer expected to see; two specific product capabilities were validated as aligned, but the broader full-suite overview the buyer originally asked for was deferred.

hard.ais_why:
• Demo covered Merlin AI assistant, AI-driven supplier onboarding via Smart Onboarding OCR, and QuestGenie scorecard builder - Checkers acknowledged Gartner leadership but did not push AI as a buying criterion.
• The active RFP is scoped to Supplier Incident Management (iSupplier + iRisk), with the full S2P RFP deferred to May; current state is manual Excel scorecarding, not an AI mandate.
• One recorded demo, RFP imminent - solid procurement transformation deal, but AI excitement is Zycus-pitched rather than customer-pulled.
hard.next_step: 6/22/26 GK: awaiting RFP release first week of July6/4/26 GK: Leslie response - S2P release pushed to beginning of Q3, we will still be included5/26/26 GK: follow up with all 3 contacts at C&R, status of SIM award and S2P RFP release4/13/26 GK: connecting with Checkers team on release date/timeline of S2P RFP3/30/26 GK: S2P RFP release end of April3/17/26 GK: followed up regarding SIM integrations and commitment to S2P rfp in following month3/12/26 GK: connect with SC team - fitment gaps too high, no longer responding to this RFP. but staying engaged with C&R for immiment S2P RFP release in 1 month. sharing integration capabilities with Supplier Incident Mgmt solutions3/2/26 GK: RFP received. beginning submission and coordinating necessary next steps2/26/26 GK: demo occurred. rfp release 3/2, plans to release full S2P rfp in May 20262/23/26 GK: prep call w/ Srishti Tuesday2/17/26 GK: scheduling pre-rfp demo for 2/26

value.name: Status quo (manual Excel/email process)
value.quote: Incident and supplier performance tracked manually in Excel scorecards until system implementation; stores report incidents via a web-based task system that emails a predefined chain, and replies outside that system are lost.
value.how_we_win: Demonstrate the dynamic, customizable incident workflow and KPI/scorecard builder shown in the demo directly against the specific gaps described (lost email replies, manual scorecarding) once the buyer re-engages.

value.name: Status quo (manual Excel/email process)
